info.name: Library System Api
info.description:
# A REST API for managing a library system built with Node.js,

Express, and MongoDB.

The system supports two user roles — students and attendants.  
Attendants manage authors, books, and borrow transactions.  
Students can view authors and books.

Base URL: {{baseUrl}}

Authentication: All protected routes require a Bearer token  
obtained from the Login endpoint. Set the token as an  
environment variable after login — it is automatically  
captured via the Login request test script.

item.name: Auth

request.description:
Creates a new student account.

Role: Public

Method: POST

URL: /api/auth/register

Pass name, email, password, and role as "student" in the  
request body. Returns the created user's id, name, email,  
and role on success.

Possible errors:

- 400: All fields are required
    
- 400: User already exists

Creates a new attendant account.

Role: Public  
Method: POST  
URL: /api/auth/register

Pass name, email, password, and role as "attendant" in the  
request body. Returns the created user's id, name, email,  
and role on success.

Possible errors:

- 400: All fields are required
    
- 400: User already exists

Authenticates a user and returns a JWT token.

Role: Public  
Method: POST  
URL: /api/auth/login

Pass email and password in the request body. On success,  
returns a JWT token and basic user details. The token is  
automatically saved to the environment variable "token"  
via the Tests script — no manual copying needed.The token expires based on the server's JWT_EXPIRES_IN  
configuration. Re-run this request when the token expires.Possible errors:

- 400: All fields are required
    
- 401: Invalid credentials

item.description:
Handles user registration and authentication.  
All routes in this folder are public — no token required.

item.name: Authors

request.description:
Creates a new author record.

Role: Attendant only  
Method: POST  
URL: /api/authors

Pass name and an optional bio in the request body.  
The author's generated ID should be copied and used  
in the authors array when creating a book.

Possible errors:

- 400: Name is required
    
- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 403: Forbidden, insufficient permissions

response.name: Create Author

request.description:
Retrieves a list of all authors in the database.

Role: Student, Attendant  
Method: GET  
URL: /api/authors

No request body needed. Returns an array of all author  
documents including id, name, bio, and timestamps.

Possible errors:

- 401: Unauthorized, token missing

response.name: Get All Authors

request.description:
Retrieves a single author by their ID.

Role: Student, Attendant  
Method: GET  
URL: /api/authors/:id

Pass the author's ID as a URL parameter. Returns the  
author document if found.

Possible errors:

- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 404: Author not found

response.name: Get Author By Id

request.description:
Updates an existing author's details.

Role: Attendant only  
Method: PUT  
URL: /api/authors/:id

Pass the author's ID as a URL parameter. Include any  
fields to update in the request body — name, bio, or both.  
Only the fields provided will be updated.

Possible errors:

- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 403: Forbidden, insufficient permissions
    
- 404: Author not found

response.name: Update Author

request.description:
Deletes an author record from the database.

Role: Attendant only  
Method: DELETE  
URL: /api/authors/:id

Pass the author's ID as a URL parameter. No request  
body needed.

Note: Deleting an author does not automatically remove  
their reference from existing books. Handle this at the  
application level if needed.

Possible errors:

- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 403: Forbidden, insufficient permissions
    
- 404: Author not found

item.description:
Manages author records in the system. Authors must exist  
in the database before they can be assigned to a book.

All routes in this folder require authentication.  
Create, update, and delete are restricted to attendants only.

item.name: Books

request.description:
Adds a new book to the library inventory.

Role: Attendant only  
Method: POST  
URL: /api/books

Pass title, isbn, an array of valid author IDs,  
totalCopies, and availableCopies in the request body.  
ISBN must be unique across all books.

Possible errors:

- 400: All required fields must be provided
    
- 400: ISBN already exists
    
- 400: At least one author is required
    
- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 403: Forbidden, insufficient permissions

Retrieves a list of all books in the library.

Role: Student, Attendant  
Method: GET  
URL: /api/books

No request body needed. Returns an array of all book  
documents with author details populated.

Possible errors:

- 401: Unauthorized, token missing

Retrieves a single book by its ID.

Role: Student, Attendant  
Method: GET  
URL: /api/books/:id

Pass the book's ID as a URL parameter. Returns the full  
book document with author details populated.

Possible errors:

- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 404: Book not found

response.name: Get Book By Id

request.description:
Updates an existing book's details.

Role: Attendant only  
Method: PUT  
URL: /api/books/:id

Pass the book's ID as a URL parameter. Include any  
fields to update in the request body. Only the fields  
provided will be updated.

Possible errors:

- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 403: Forbidden, insufficient permissions
    
- 404: Book not found

Deletes a book from the library inventory.

Role: Attendant only  
Method: DELETE  
URL: /api/books/:id

Pass the book's ID as a URL parameter. No request  
body needed.

Note: A book with active borrow records should not  
be deleted. Verify no active borrows exist before  
deleting.

Possible errors:

- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 403: Forbidden, insufficient permissions
    
- 404: Book not found

item.description:
Manages the library's book inventory. Books must be  
created with at least one valid author ID that already  
exists in the database.

All routes require authentication. Add, update, and  
delete are restricted to attendants only.

item.name: Borrows

request.description:
Issues a book to a student and creates a borrow record.

Role: Attendant only  
Method: POST  
URL: /api/borrows

Pass bookId and studentId in the request body. The  
attendant's ID is automatically extracted from the  
authentication token — it does not need to be passed  
in the request body.

On success:

- A new BorrowRecord is created with status "borrowed"
    
- The book's availableCopies is decremented by 1
    

Possible errors:

- 400: bookId and studentId are required
    
- 400: Book is not available for borrowing
    
- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 403: Forbidden, insufficient permissions
    
- 404: Book not found
    
- 404: Student not found

Processes a book return and updates the borrow record.

Role: Attendant only  
Method: PUT  
URL: /api/borrows/:borrowId/return

Pass the borrowId as a URL parameter. No request  
body needed. The system automatically sets the  
returnDate to the current date and updates the  
borrow status.

On success:

- BorrowRecord status is updated to "returned"
    
- returnDate is set to current date
    
- Book's availableCopies is incremented by 1
    
- Book status is set back to "IN" if availableCopies  
    goes above 0
    

Possible errors:

- 401: Unauthorized, token missing
    
- 403: Forbidden, insufficient permissions
    
- 404: Borrow record not found
    
- 400: Book has already been returned

item.description:
Manages all borrow and return transactions in the system.  
Every borrow creates a new BorrowRecord and updates the  
book's availability. Every return updates the BorrowRecord  
and restores the book's availability.

All routes require authentication and are restricted  
to attendants only.
